title: thymus praecox var. laniger
intro: Thymus praecox var. laniger, also known as creeping thyme, is a low-growing, spreading perennial herb native to Europe, with tiny leaves and pink to purple flowers. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly as an antiseptic, antifungal, and antimicrobial agent, making it useful in treating wounds and skin infections. The health benefits of creeping thyme include its ability to reduce stress, improve respiratory issues, and boost the immune system, thanks to its high content of essential oils and flavonoids. Creeping thyme can be prepared as an herbal tea, infused oil, or added to salves and creams for topical use, making it a popular choice in herbal medicine.
